active_items.final_wording: People have the right to be involved in discussions and make informed decisions about their care, as described in NICE's information on making decisions about your care . Making decisions using NICE guidelines explains how we use words to show the strength (or certainty) of our recommendations, and has information about prescribing medicines (including off-label use), professional guidelines, standards and laws (including on consent and mental capacity), and safeguarding.

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Offer patients and carers clear, consistent information and advice throughout all stages of their care. This should include the risks of surgical site infections , what is being done to reduce them and how they are managed. For more guidance on providing information to adults and discussing their preferences with them, see the NICE guideline on patient experience in adult NHS services . [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer patients and carers clear, consistent information and advice throughout all stages of their care. This should include the risks of surgical site infections , what is being done to reduce them and how they are managed. For more guidance on providing information to adults and discussing their preferences with them, see the NICE guideline on patient experience in adult NHS services . [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Offer patients and carers information and advice on how to care for their wound after discharge. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer patients and carers information and advice on how to care for their wound after discharge. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Offer patients and carers information and advice about how to recognise a surgical site infection and who to contact if they are concerned. Use an integrated care pathway for healthcare-associated infections to help communicate this information to both patients and all those involved in their care after discharge. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Offer patients and carers information and advice about how to recognise a surgical site infection and who to contact if they are concerned. Use an integrated care pathway for healthcare-associated infections to help communicate this information to both patients and all those involved in their care after discharge. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Always inform patients after their operation if they have been given antibiotics. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Always inform patients after their operation if they have been given antibiotics. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Advise patients to shower or have a bath (or help patients to shower, bath or bed bath) using soap, either the day before, or on the day of, surgery. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Advise patients to shower or have a bath (or help patients to shower, bath or bed bath) using soap, either the day before, or on the day of, surgery. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Consider nasal mupirocin in combination with a chlorhexidine body wash before procedures in which Staphylococcus aureus is a likely cause of a surgical site infection. This should be locally determined and take into account: the type of procedure individual patient risk factors the increased risk of side effects in preterm infants (see the recommendation on risks in babies in the section on antiseptic skin preparation ) the potential impact of infection. [2019]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Maintain surveillance on antimicrobial resistance associated with the use of mupirocin. For information on antimicrobial stewardship programmes, see the NICE guideline on antimicrobial stewardship: systems and processes for effective antimicrobial medicine use . [2019] For a short explanation of why the committee made the 2019 recommendations and how they might affect practice, see the rationale and impact section on nasal decolonisation . Full details of the evidence and the committee's discussion are in evidence review A: nasal decontamination in the prevention of surgical site infection .

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Do not use hair removal routinely to reduce the risk of surgical site infection. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not use hair removal routinely to reduce the risk of surgical site infection. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: If hair has to be removed, use electric clippers with a single-use head on the day of surgery. Do not use razors for hair removal, because they increase the risk of surgical site infection. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If hair has to be removed, use electric clippers with a single-use head on the day of surgery. Do not use razors for hair removal, because they increase the risk of surgical site infection. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Give patients specific theatre wear that is appropriate for the procedure and clinical setting, and that provides easy access to the operative site and areas for placing devices, such as intravenous cannulas. Take into account the patient's comfort and dignity. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Give patients specific theatre wear that is appropriate for the procedure and clinical setting, and that provides easy access to the operative site and areas for placing devices, such as intravenous cannulas. Take into account the patient's comfort and dignity. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: All staff should wear specific non-sterile theatre wear in all areas where operations are undertaken. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: All staff should wear specific non-sterile theatre wear in all areas where operations are undertaken. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Staff wearing non-sterile theatre wear should keep their movements in and out of the operating area to a minimum. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Staff wearing non-sterile theatre wear should keep their movements in and out of the operating area to a minimum. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Do not use mechanical bowel preparation routinely to reduce the risk of surgical site infection. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not use mechanical bowel preparation routinely to reduce the risk of surgical site infection. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: The operating team should remove hand jewellery before operations. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
Hand jewellery, artificial nails and nail polish
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: The operating team should remove hand jewellery before operations. [2008]

active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: The operating team should remove artificial nails and nail polish before operations. [2008]

Hand jewellery, artificial nails and nail polish

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Give antibiotic prophylaxis to patients before: clean surgery involving the placement of a prosthesis or implant clean-contaminated surgery contaminated surgery . [2008] For advice on antibiotic prophylaxis before caesarean birth, see the section on timing of antibiotic administration in NICE's guideline on caesarean birth . For information on antimicrobial stewardship programmes see the NICE guideline on antimicrobial stewardship: systems and processes for effective antimicrobial medicine use .

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Give antibiotic prophylaxis to patients before: clean surgery involving the placement of a prosthesis or implant clean-contaminated surgery contaminated surgery . [2008] For advice on antibiotic prophylaxis before caesarean birth, see the section on timing of antibiotic administration in NICE's guideline on caesarean birth . For information on antimicrobial stewardship programmes see the NICE guid

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Do not use antibiotic prophylaxis routinely for clean non-prosthetic uncomplicated surgery. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not use antibiotic prophylaxis routinely for clean non-prosthetic uncomplicated surgery. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Use the local antibiotic formulary and always take into account the potential adverse effects when choosing specific antibiotics for prophylaxis. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Use the local antibiotic formulary and always take into account the potential adverse effects when choosing specific antibiotics for prophylaxis. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Consider giving a single dose of antibiotic prophylaxis intravenously on starting anaesthesia. However, give prophylaxis earlier for operations in which a tourniquet is used. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider giving a single dose of antibiotic prophylaxis intravenously on starting anaesthesia. However, give prophylaxis earlier for operations in which a tourniquet is used. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Before giving antibiotic prophylaxis, take into account the timing and pharmacokinetics (for example, the serum half-life) and necessary infusion time of the antibiotic. Give a repeat dose of antibiotic prophylaxis when the operation is longer than the half-life of the antibiotic given. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Before giving antibiotic prophylaxis, take into account the timing and pharmacokinetics (for example, the serum half-life) and necessary infusion time of the antibiotic. Give a repeat dose of antibiotic prophylaxis when the operation is longer than the half-life of the antibiotic given. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Give antibiotic treatment (in addition to prophylaxis) to patients having surgery on a dirty or infected wound . [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Give antibiotic treatment (in addition to prophylaxis) to patients having surgery on a dirty or infected wound . [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Inform patients before the operation, whenever possible, if they will need antibiotic prophylaxis, and afterwards if they have been given antibiotics during their operation. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Inform patients before the operation, whenever possible, if they will need antibiotic prophylaxis, and afterwards if they have been given antibiotics during their operation. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: The operating team should wash their hands prior to the first operation on the list using an aqueous antiseptic surgical solution, with a single-use brush or pick for the nails, and ensure that hands and nails are visibly clean. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: The operating team should wash their hands prior to the first operation on the list using an aqueous antiseptic surgical solution, with a single-use brush or pick for the nails, and ensure that hands and nails are visibly clean. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Before subsequent operations, hands should be washed using either an alcoholic hand rub or an antiseptic surgical solution. If hands are soiled then they should be washed again with an antiseptic surgical solution. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Before subsequent operations, hands should be washed using either an alcoholic hand rub or an antiseptic surgical solution. If hands are soiled then they should be washed again with an antiseptic surgical solution. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Do not use non-iodophor-impregnated incise drapes routinely for surgery as they may increase the risk of surgical site infection. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not use non-iodophor-impregnated incise drapes routinely for surgery as they may increase the risk of surgical site infection. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: If an incise drape is required, use an iodophor-impregnated drape unless the patient has an iodine allergy. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If an incise drape is required, use an iodophor-impregnated drape unless the patient has an iodine allergy. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: The operating team should wear sterile gowns in the operating theatre during the operation. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: The operating team should wear sterile gowns in the operating theatre during the operation. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Consider wearing 2 pairs of sterile gloves when there is a high risk of glove perforation and the consequences of contamination may be serious. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Consider wearing 2 pairs of sterile gloves when there is a high risk of glove perforation and the consequences of contamination may be serious. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Prepare the skin at the surgical site immediately before incision using an antiseptic preparation. [2019]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Prepare the skin at the surgical site immediately before incision using an antiseptic preparation. [2019]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Be aware of the risks of using skin antiseptics in babies, in particular the risk of severe chemical injuries with the use of chlorhexidine (both alcohol-based and aqueous solutions) in preterm babies. [2019]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Be aware of the risks of using skin antiseptics in babies, in particular the risk of severe chemical injuries with the use of chlorhexidine (both alcohol-based and aqueous solutions) in preterm babies. [2019]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: When deciding which antiseptic skin preparation to use, options may include those in table 1. [2019] Table 1 Options for antiseptic skin preparation When Choice of antiseptic skin preparation First choice unless contraindicated or the surgical site is next to a mucous membrane Alcohol-based solution of chlorhexidine Alternative if the surgical site is next to a mucous membrane Aqueous solution of chlorhexidine Alternative if chlorhexidine is contraindicated Alcohol-based solution of povidone-iodine If both an alcohol-based solution and chlorhexidine are unsuitable Aqueous solution of povidone-iodine Some products may not be licenced for surgical site antiseptic skin preparation, check individual SPCs and NICE's information on prescribing medicines .

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: When deciding which antiseptic skin preparation to use, options may include those in table 1. active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: If diathermy is to be carried out: use evaporation to dry antiseptic skin preparations and avoid pooling of alcohol-based preparations. [2019] For a short explanation of why the committee made the 2019 recommendations and how they might affect practice, see the rationale and impact section on antiseptic skin preparation . Full details of the evidence and the committee's discussion are in evidence review B: skin antiseptics in the prevention of surgical site infection .

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: If diathermy is to be carried out: use evaporation to dry antiseptic skin preparations and avoid pooling of alcohol-based preparations. Full details of the evidence and the committee's discussion are in evidence

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Do not use diathermy for surgical incision to reduce the risk of surgical site infection. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not use diathermy for surgical incision to reduce the risk of surgical site infection. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Maintain patient temperature in line with NICE's guideline on hypothermia: prevention and management in adults having surgery . [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Maintain patient temperature in line with NICE's guideline on hypothermia: prevention and management in adults having surgery . [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Maintain optimal oxygenation during surgery. In particular, give patients sufficient oxygen during major surgery and in the recovery period to ensure that a haemoglobin saturation of more than 95% is maintained. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Maintain optimal oxygenation during surgery. In particular, give patients sufficient oxygen during major surgery and in the recovery period to ensure that a haemoglobin saturation of more than 95% is maintained. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Maintain adequate perfusion during surgery. See additional recommendations on intravenous fluids and cardiac monitoring for adults in NICE's guideline on perioperative care in adults . [2008, amended 2020]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Maintain adequate perfusion during surgery. See additional recommendations on intravenous fluids and cardiac monitoring for adults in NICE's guideline on perioperative care in adults . [2008, amended 2020]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Do not give insulin routinely to patients who do not have diabetes to optimise blood glucose postoperatively as a means of reducing the risk of surgical site infection. See the additional recommendation on blood glucose control for adults in NICE's guideline on perioperative care in adults . [2008, amended 2020]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not give insulin routinely to patients who do not have diabetes to optimise blood glucose postoperatively as a means of reducing the risk of surgical site infection. See the additional recommendation on blood glucose control for adults in NICE's guideline on perioperative care in adults . [2008, amended 2020]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Do not use wound irrigation to reduce the risk of surgical site infection. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not use wound irrigation to reduce the risk of surgical site infection. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Do not use intracavity lavage to reduce the risk of surgical site infection. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Do not use intracavity lavage to reduce the risk of surgical site infection. [2008]

active_items.final_wording: When using sutures, consider using antimicrobial triclosan-coated sutures, especially for paediatric surgery, to reduce the risk of surgical site infection. [2019]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: When using sutures, consider using antimicrobial triclosan-coated sutures, especially for paediatric surgery, to reduce the risk of surgical site infection. [2019]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Consider using sutures rather than staples to close the skin after caesarean birth to reduce the risk of superficial wound dehiscence. [2019] For a short explanation of why the committee made the 2019 recommendations and how they might affect practice, see the rationale and impact section on closure methods . Full details of the evidence and the committee's discussion are in evidence review D: closure materials and techniques in the prevention of surgical site infection .

active_items.final_wording: Do not use topical antimicrobial agents for surgical wounds that are healing by primary intention to reduce the risk of surgical site infection. [2008]

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: When surgical site infection is suspected by the presence of cellulitis, either by a new infection or an infection caused by treatment failure, give the patient an antibiotic that covers the likely causative organisms. Consider local resistance patterns and the results of microbiological tests in choosing an antibiotic. For information on antimicrobial stewardship programmes see the NICE guideline on antimicrobial stewardship: systems and processes for effective antimicrobial medicine use . [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
Antibiotic treatment of surgical site infection and treatment failure

active_items.final_wording: Do not use Eusol and gauze, or dextranomer or enzymatic treatments for debridement in the management of surgical site infection. [2008]

active_items.final_wording: Use a structured approach to care to improve overall management of surgical wounds. This should include preoperative assessments to identify people with potential wound healing problems. Enhanced education of healthcare workers, patients and carers, and sharing of clinical expertise is needed to support this. [2008]

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Use a structured approach to care to improve overall management of surgical wounds. Enhanced education of healthcare workers, patients and carers, and sharing of clinical expertise is needed to support this. [2008]

active_items.final_wording: The process of eradicating or reducing asymptomatic carriage of methicillin-resistant S. aureus (MRSA). This used to be referred to as decontamination.

active_items.final_wording: Occurs when a wound has been sutured after an operation and heals to leave a minimal, cosmetically acceptable scar.

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment
exact_locator.paragraph_or_text_span: Occurs when a wound has been sutured after an operation and heals to leave a minimal, cosmetically acceptable scar.

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: Occurs when a wound is deliberately left open at the end of an operation because of excessive bacterial contamination, particularly by anaerobes or when there is a risk of devitalised tissue, which leads to infection and delayed healing. It may be sutured within a few days (delayed primary closure), or much later when the wound is clean and granulating (secondary closure), or left to complete healing naturally without suturing.

active_items.final_wording: Dressings designed to promote the wound healing process through the creation and maintenance of a local, warm, moist environment underneath the chosen dressing, when left in place for a period indicated through a continuous assessment process.

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment

active_items.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
active_items.setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
active_items.rationale: Directly reproduced from an exact evidence statement in a complete archetype-compatible pack.

active_items.final_wording: A surgical wound with local signs and symptoms of infection, for example, heat, redness, pain and swelling, and (in more serious cases) with systemic signs of fever or a raised white blood cell count. Infection in the surgical wound may prevent healing, causing the wound edges to separate, or it may cause an abscess to form in the deeper tissues. Definitions of the severity of surgical site infections vary and this should be taken into account when comparing reported rates of surgical site infection.

heading_path: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment

active_items.final_wording: Clean: an incision in which no inflammation is encountered in a surgical procedure, without a break in sterile technique, and during which the respiratory, alimentary or genitourinary tracts are not entered. Clean-contaminated: an incision through which the respiratory, alimentary, or genitourinary tract is entered under controlled conditions but with no contamination encountered. Contaminated: an incision undertaken during an operation in which there is a major break in sterile technique or gross spillage from the gastrointestinal tract, or an incision in which acute, non-purulent inflammation is encountered. Open traumatic wounds that are more than 12 to 24 hours old also fall into this category.
